definition.en: Expectation is behind all Sense data, and forms it.
definition.zh: 期待位于所有感官资料背后，并塑造了它。

definition.en: Expectation is actually the main trigger that switches inner data into the realm of physical construction.
definition.zh: 期待实际上是主要触发器，将内在资料切换到物质建构的领域。

definition.en: Expectation is the force, then, that triggers psychic realities into physical construction.
definition.zh: 期待是那股力量，触发心灵实相进入物质建构。

definition.en: Your physical condition in every way is a reflection of your inner expectations, accomplishments, and failures.
definition.zh: 你的身体状况在各方面都是你内在期待、成就与失败的反映。

definition.en: Expectation is somewhat influenced also by past existences, and yet not enough to be binding upon the present personality.
definition.zh: 期待也多少受到过去存在的影响，但不足以束缚当前的人格。

explanation:
期望是内在数据转化为物理现实的触发器。光有欲望不够，必须要有期望——欲望可能发展为期望，但单独存在时无法产生任何物理建构。期望塑造能力：人们常说期望超出能力，但实际上是期望形成能力，期望越高，能力越茁壮。

期望是所有感官数据背后的驱动力，直接塑造你感知到的物质环境。你的身体状况、财务状况、人际关系，无一不是内在期望的反映。你只会建构出你相信能够建构的环境。期望若错误，当自我休息时，你可以在梦中重建期望高昂的时刻，从而打破不良期望的恶性循环。

期望能在潜意识层面调动强大的能力与理解力。对危险的期望会创造危险，对成功的期望会创造成功。期望还影响你如何筛选和解读接收到的内在数据。

quotes.text: However, with the ego at rest the individual may allow communications and dream constructions through, past the ego barrier, in such a manner that he becomes in some ways free. If for example his present expectations are faulty, when the ego rests he may recreate a time when expectations were high. The resulting dream will then partially break the circle of poor expectations, with their shoddy physical constructions, and start such an individual along a more beneficial path. In other words, such a dream may begin to transform the physical environment through lifting inner expectation.
quotes.translation: 然而，当自我处于休息状态时，个体可能允许沟通和梦境建构通过自我屏障，以某种方式获得自由。例如，如果他目前的期望有误，当自我休息时，他可能重新创造一个期望高涨的时刻。由此产生的梦境将部分打破不良期望及其劣质物质建构的循环，引导个体走上更有利的道路。换句话说，这样的梦境可能通过提升内在期望开始转变物质环境。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: While many of your expectations are formed in childhood, no switch is really stuck in one position, and it is your prerogative to channel your emotional energy into whatever pattern for action you desire. It is extremely important, if difficult, to probe and to discover exactly what your present expectations are. Not your desires but your expectations, for you will only construct physically that environment which you believe capable of construction. It has been said that oftentimes men's expectations are too high for their abilities, but indeed expectations form abilities: and if expectations were higher, so would abilities flourish.
quotes.translation: 虽然你的许多期望形成于童年，但没有任何开关真的固定在一个位置，你有权将自己的情感能量引导到任何你想要的行动模式中。探索并准确发现你目前的期望是什么极为重要，尽管这很困难。不是你的欲望，而是你的期望，因为你只会建构你相信能够建构的物质环境。常有人说人们的期望往往超出能力，但事实上期望塑造能力：如果期望更高，能力也会蓬勃发展。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: This is one of the reasons why health suggestions given immediately before sleep are so effective. Incidentally, they are excellent practice. Expectation can also aid you. In this case suggestions should be given leading to dreams in which a desired situation is accomplished. The dream will then increase and activate your own expectations. And as you know, the expectations themselves have much to do with whether or not any given situation will occur. Further detailed discussions will be held on all of these points, before these matters are covered.
quotes.translation: 这就是为什么睡前立即给予的健康建议如此有效的原因之一。顺便说一句，这些是极好的练习。期望也能帮助你。在这种情况下，应给予引导至实现理想情境的梦境的建议。梦境随后会增强并激活你自己的期望。如你所知，期望本身与任何特定情境是否会发生有很大关系。在涉及这些事项之前，将对所有这些要点进行更详细的讨论。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: You must still deal with physical reality, for it is definitely the representation of inner data. I have said this constantly: Expectation is behind all Sense data, and forms it. I have told you--and incidentally my lovely skeptics I have told you in the past--that you create your own physical environment and universe. Your physical condition in every way is a reflection of your inner expectations, accomplishments, and failures, and I have told you that any illness is the result of an inner distortion.
quotes.translation: 你仍然必须处理物质实相，因为它确实是内在数据的表征。我一直这样说：期望是所有感官数据背后的驱动力，并塑造它。我告诉过你——顺便说一句，我可爱的怀疑者们，我过去告诉过你们——你创造了自己的物质环境和宇宙。你的身体状况在各方面都是你内在期望、成就和失败的反映，我告诉过你，任何疾病都是内在扭曲的结果。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: In other respects of course there have been profound changes. By your attitude you are effectively forming a barrier against any financial betterment of any significant kind. You repulse the circumstances in which such betterment could take place automatically, because of the effect of your expectations. It goes without saying that a fulltime job would better the financial situation, but this is not the sort of thing to which I am referring. Your situation could be bettered financially while you retain basically the same schedule you now enjoy, if--and a very large if--if changed expectations were creating the psychic climate in which supply would flow. Doors would open that you do not know existed.
quotes.translation: 当然在其他方面已有深刻变化。通过你的态度，你有效地形成了阻碍任何重大财务改善的屏障。由于你期望的影响，你排斥了这种改善可能自动发生的情境。不用说，全职工作会改善财务状况，但这不是我指的那种情况。如果你基本上保持目前享受的相同日程，你的财务状况也可能得到改善，如果——这是一个很大的如果——如果改变的期望正在创造供应流动的心灵气候。你未知的门将会打开。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: If a man wants to change his fate, desire is not enough, but expectation is. Desire may grow into expectation, but alone it is not enough. Expectation is actually the main trigger that switches inner data into the realm of physical construction. Without it, no physical construction results.
quotes.translation: 如果一个人想改变命运，欲望是不够的，但期望足够。欲望可能成长为期望，但仅凭欲望不够。期望实际上是触发内在数据转化为物质建构领域的主要扳机。没有它，就不会产生物质建构。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: Love, therefore, can contain hate very nicely. Hatred can contain love and be driven by it, particularly by an idealized love. (Pause.) You "hate" something that separates you from a loved object. It is precisely because the object is loved that it is so disliked if expectations are not met. You may love a parent, and if the parent does not seem to return the love and denies your expectations, then you may "hate" the same parent because of the love that leads you to expect more. The hatred is meant to get you your love back. It is supposed to lead to a communication from you, stating your feelings -- clearing the air, so to speak, and bringing you closer to the love object. Hatred is not the denial of love, then, but an attempt to regain it, and a painful recognition of circumstances that separate you from it.
quotes.translation: 因此，爱可以很好地容纳恨。恨也可以包含爱，并被爱所驱动，尤其是被理想化的爱所驱动。（停顿。）你“恨”那些将你与所爱对象分离的事物。正因为对象是被爱的，所以当期望未能实现时，它才会如此令人不悦。你可能爱一位父母，如果这位父母似乎没有回报你的爱，并辜负了你的期望，那么你可能会“恨”同一位父母，因为爱让你期望更多。恨意在本质上是想让你重新获得爱。它本应引导你进行沟通，表达你的感受——可以说是澄清局面，让你更接近所爱的对象。因此，恨并非对爱的否定，而是试图重新获得爱，并痛苦地认识到那些将你与爱分离的境况。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: I will, then, begin with a short treatise concerning the importance of expectation, not only in the construction of physical objects from inner data, but also in the importance played by expectation in the actual sifting of inner data that is received, and in the importance of expectation in the interpretation of inner data after the sifting process has been carried about.
quotes.translation: 那么，我将从一篇关于期望重要性的简短论述开始，不仅涉及从内在数据构建物理对象的过程，还涉及期望在接收内在数据时对实际筛选过程的重要性，以及在筛选过程完成后，期望在解释内在数据时的重要性。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: Suggestion is therefore one of the characteristics of action. The term suggestion is a poor one. As it operates within your system, and within the human personality, the word expectation is a much better term. Nevertheless, expectation is only one phase, for the same kind of inner directive activity is pertinent within all forms of action.
quotes.translation: 因此，暗示是行动的特性之一。“暗示”这个术语并不恰当。在你们的系统内以及人类人格中，“期望”这个词是更好的术语。然而，期望只是其中一个阶段，因为同类的内在指导活动在所有形式的行动中都是相关的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

source_raw.title: （In Volume I of The Early Sessions this sensation, as I call it, is dealt with following Session 23, in Session 24, and following Session 25, among others. I have experienced it frequently during psychological time since then. It is much richer and more varied than the more usual thrilling one notes upon a moving occasion. In the above instance, the initial source of the sensation appeared to stem from outside the body, and the waves against my ears had a tangible, solid quality that is difficult to describe.

quotes.text: An expectation of danger will indeed create danger. An expectation of success will create success. This is put very simply and yet there is nothing, in practical terms, more valid, since expectation has behind it the motivating force of the personality, and utilizes on a subconscious level strong abilities and comprehensions.
quotes.translation: 对危险的期望确实会创造危险。对成功的期望会创造成功。这说得非常简单，但在实际层面上，没有比这更有效的了，因为期望背后有人格的驱动力量，并在潜意识层面上利用了强大的能力和理解力。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: I would state furthermore that indeed Ruburt did have occasion to be angry at the chiropractor, since with an emotional fear unthinking suggestions such as his, made with only the flimsiest of evidence, can be most harmful and destructive. And in an unwary, emotionally upset personality, particularly if under stress, such a suggestion could cause a harmless and protective nodule to be changed by the strong powers of adverse expectation, or rather expectation poorly used, into the form of what is feared; as a slight but harmless irregularity of heartbeat, with the unthinking suggestion of a doctor, can become through the patient's fears an actual functional disorder, so could suggestion turn a relatively harmless formation like Ruburt's into an arthritic condition.
quotes.translation: 我还要进一步说明，鲁柏确实有理由对那位脊椎按摩师生气，因为像他那样基于情感恐惧、仅凭最微弱的证据就做出的轻率暗示，可能非常有害且具有破坏性。在一个粗心大意、情绪不稳定的人格中，尤其是在压力之下，这样的暗示可能会通过不良期望（或者说使用不当的期望）的强大力量，将一个无害且具有保护性的结节转变为所恐惧的形式；就像医生轻率的暗示，可能通过患者的恐惧，将轻微但无害的心律不齐转变为实际的功能性障碍一样，暗示也可能将像鲁柏那样相对无害的形态转变为关节炎的状况。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Now, you see what you are willing to see, and it is stupidity to consider suggestion as the result or the cause of what you see. It is stupidity in class to worry that suggestion would cause a given result... for suggestion causes whatever you see. You form your physical reality through suggestion and expectation. You experience what you expect to experience at a subconscious and a conscious level. And therefore as Ruburt is very careful that suggestion is not involved, so he has also had you be overly cautious, and there have been many opportunities in class that you have missed for this reason... and these are the bets that I have spoken about earlier this evening. You have the ability to see more than you saw, and you have the ability. You enjoyed your passivity (to CW) to the point of a joyous giving-up; and instead, you see, there is a point within passivity where you are passively alert. And you went beyond the point and lost what you might have seen. As our friend here went beyond the point (referring to TM), looked at the portraits and consciously... for you did not make an original intuitive judgment... but consciously looked at those portraits in terms of nationality, age, and all the requirements that you thought of.
quotes.translation: 现在，你明白你只愿意看到你想看到的，而将暗示视为你所见之物的结果或原因是愚蠢的。在课堂上担心暗示会导致特定结果也是愚蠢的……因为暗示会导致你所见的一切。你通过暗示和期望形成你的物质实相。你在潜意识和意识层面上体验你所期望体验的。因此，正如鲁柏非常小心地避免暗示介入一样，他也让你变得过于谨慎，因此你错过了课堂上许多机会……这就是我今晚早些时候提到的那些赌注。你有能力看到比你实际看到的更多，你确实有这个能力。你享受你的被动性（对CW说）到了愉快放弃的地步；然而，你看，在被动性中有一个点，你在那里是被动警觉的。而你越过了那个点，失去了你可能看到的东西。就像我们这位朋友（指TM）越过了那个点，看着那些肖像，并自觉地……因为你没有做出原始的直觉判断……而是自觉地根据国籍、年龄以及你想到的所有要求来看那些肖像。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: I am not a physical personality. Basically, however, neither are you. Your experience now is physical. You are a creator translating your expectations into physical form. The world is meant to serve as a reference point. The exterior appearance is a replica of inner desire. You can change your personal world. You do change it without knowing it. You have only to use your ability consciously, to examine the nature of your thoughts and feelings and project those with which you basically agree.
quotes.translation: 我并非一个物质人格。然而，从根本上说，你也不是。你现在的经验是物质的。你是一个创造者，将你的期望转化为物质形态。世界被设计成一个参考点。外在的表象是内在渴望的复制品。你可以改变你的个人世界。你确实在不知不觉中改变它。你只需有意识地运用你的能力，检视你思想和感受的本质，并投射出你基本认同的那些。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Hatred always involves a painful sense of separation from love, which may be idealized. A person you feel strongly against at any given time upsets you because he or she does not live up to your expectations. The higher your expectations the greater any divergence from them seems. If you hate a parent it is precisely because you expect such love. A person from whom you expect nothing will never earn your bitterness.
quotes.translation: 仇恨总是涉及一种与爱的痛苦分离感，而爱可能被理想化。在任何特定时刻，你强烈反对的人之所以让你不安，是因为他或她未能达到你的期望。你的期望越高，任何偏离就显得越大。如果你恨一位父母，那恰恰是因为你期望如此的爱。一个你对其毫无期望的人，永远不会赢得你的怨恨。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: This will last very briefly. Heaven and hell, indeed, are mere representations. They represented originally intuitive insights. But no heaven or hell exist in those terms. There is no place within the universe or within any universe or system for them. You create reality according to your beliefs and expectations. Therefore, it behooves you to examine your beliefs and expectations very clearly.
quotes.translation: 这只会持续很短的时间。天堂和地狱，确实只是表象。它们最初代表直觉的洞见。但以那些术语来说，并不存在天堂或地狱。宇宙内，或任何宇宙或系统内，都没有它们的位置。你根据你的信念和期望创造实相。因此，你应当非常清晰地检视你的信念和期望。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: This is extremely valuable information, particularly concerning the part played by expectation in the sifting of available data. Expectation is somewhat influenced also by past existences, and yet not enough to be binding upon the present personality.
quotes.translation: 这是极其宝贵的信息，特别是关于期望在筛选可用数据中所扮演的角色。期望也受到过去存在的一定影响，但不足以束缚当前的人格。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: Each person alive helps paint the living picture of civilization as it exists at any given time, in your terms. Be your own best artist. Your thoughts, feelings and expectations are like the living brush strokes with which you paint your corner of life's landscape. If you do your best in your own life, then you are indeed helping to improve the quality of all life. Your thoughts are as real as snowflakes or raindrops or clouds. They mix and merge with the thoughts of others, to form man's livingscape, providing the vast mental elements from which physical events will be formed.
quotes.translation: 每个活着的人，都帮助描绘了文明在你们所说的任何特定时刻存在的活生生的画面。做你自己最好的艺术家。你的思想、感受和期望，就像你用来描绘生命风景中自己角落的活生生的笔触。如果你在自己的生命中尽力而为，那么你确实在帮助改善所有生命的质量。你的思想就像雪花、雨滴或云朵一样真实。它们与他人的思想混合并融合，形成人类的生命景观，提供物质事件将从中形成的广阔心灵元素。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: I have told you they were fragments, and if you will reread the material in the light of the newer material on matter, then the whole incident will become more plain to you. I also will tie these discussions in with their strong connection with expectation, as the involvement here is extremely practical in everyday terms; and though it may seem at first to be Pollyanna, it certainly is not, and in many cases much training is necessary before proper attitudes can be achieved. In all cases without exception, fear leads to defective constructions, and the cycle is vicious.
quotes.translation: 我告诉过你它们是碎片，如果你根据关于物质的新材料重读这些资料，那么整个事件对你来说会变得更加清晰。我也会将这些讨论与它们与期望的强烈联系结合起来，因为这里的关联在日常用语中极其实用；虽然起初可能显得像波莉安娜，但当然不是，而且在许多情况下，需要大量训练才能达到适当的态度。在所有情况下，无一例外，恐惧导致有缺陷的构建，而循环是恶性的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)

quotes.text: You always know what you are doing, even when you do not realize it. Your eye knows it sees, though it cannot see itself except through the use of reflection. In the same way the world as you see it is a reflection of what you are, a reflection not in glass but in three-dimensional reality. You project your thoughts, feelings, and expectations outward, then you perceive them as the outside reality. When it seems to you that others are observing you, you are observing yourself from the standpoint of your own projections.
quotes.translation: 你始终知道自己在做什么，即使你并未意识到。你的眼睛知道它在看，尽管它只能通过反射才能看见自身。同样地，你所见的世界是你自身的反映——不是镜中的倒影，而是三维实相中的投射。你将思想、情感与期待向外投射，随后将它们感知为外在实相。当他人似乎正在观察你时，你其实是从自身投射的立场在观察自己。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Because his conscious desire was based strongly upon inner emotional need, and not opposed to it, and because the emotional need at that time was powerful, that is his need to leave on vacation, and because he remembered our discussion on expectation, he was able to utilize both conscious and unconscious energies. In other words, to consciously focus his subconscious psychic abilities to perform toward a definite, material end.

quotes.text: It is not enough to meditate, or to imagine in your mind some desired goal being accomplished, if you are afraid to act upon the very impulses to which your meditations and imaginings give rise. When you do not take any steps toward an ideal position, then your life does lack excitement. You become depressed. You might become an idealist in reverse, so that you find a certain excitement in contemplating the occurrence of natural disasters, such as earthquakes. (Pause.) You may begin to concentrate your attention on such activities. You may contemplate the end of the world instead, but in either case you are propelled by a sense of personal frustration, and perhaps by some degree of vengeance, seeing in your mind the destruction of a world that fell so far beneath your idealized expectations.
quotes.translation: 仅靠冥想或在心中想象某个渴望的目标已经达成是不够的，如果你害怕去实践那些由冥想和想象所激发的冲动的话。当你没有朝着理想状态迈出任何步伐时，你的生活确实缺乏激情。你会变得沮丧。你可能会成为一个反向的理想主义者，以至于在思考自然灾害（如地震）的发生时找到某种兴奋感。（停顿。）你可能会开始将注意力集中在这样的活动上。你可能会转而思考世界末日，但无论哪种情况，你都被一种个人的挫败感所驱使，或许还有某种程度的报复心理，在脑海中看到一个远低于你理想化期望的世界被毁灭。

quotes.text: People want, then, to throw aside old structures of belief. They yearn, often without recognizing it, for the remembered knowledge of early childhood, when it seems that they experienced for a time a dimension of experience in which the unexpected was taken for granted, when "magical events" occurred quite naturally. They begin to look at the structure of their lives in a different fashion that attempts to evoke from nature, and from their own natures, some graceful effortlessness, some freedom nearly forgotten. They begin to turn toward a more natural and a more magical approach to their own lives. At such times the conserving elements in nature and in society itself do not seem as strong as they did before. Surprising events that were earlier covered up or ignored seem to appear with greater frequency, and everywhere a new sense of quickness and acceleration gradually alters the expectations of people in regard to the events of their own lives, and to the behavior they expect from others. You are in such times now.
quotes.translation: 于是，人们想要抛弃旧的信念结构。他们渴望——常常没有意识到——童年早期那种被记住的知识，那时他们似乎曾短暂体验过一个维度，在其中意外被视为理所当然，"神奇事件"自然而然地发生。他们开始以不同的方式审视自己生活的结构，试图从自然以及自己的本性中唤起某种优雅的轻松感，某种几乎被遗忘的自由。他们开始转向一种更自然、更神奇的方式来对待自己的生活。在这样的时刻，自然和社会中的保守元素似乎不再像以前那样强大。那些先前被掩盖或忽视的惊人事件似乎更频繁地出现，而一种新的敏捷和加速感逐渐改变了人们对自身生活事件的期望，以及他们对他人行为的预期。你们现在就处于这样的时代。

And the greater the number of such destructive physical constructions, the greater his expectation of further fear.
quotes.translation: 这种错误可能变成习惯，影响所有其他心理结构，导致不幸且危险的身体建构。这些极具破坏性的错误有许多成因。随后，外在感官将这种身体建构感知为具有威胁性且令人恐惧，并通过外在感官影响内在个体，使其开始陷入恶性循环，试图形成更多、更具威胁性的身体建构来对抗先前的建构。而这类破坏性身体建构的数量越多，他对进一步恐惧的期待就越大。

quotes.text: It may even improve. Since I have said that expectations are formed by the emotions, then it is obviously the basic emotions themselves that must be manipulated, since the expectations are the frameworks formed by the emotions. This is the starting point.
quotes.translation: 情况甚至可能改善。既然我说过期待是由情绪形成的，那么显然必须处理的是基本情绪本身，因为期待是情绪形成的框架。这就是起点。

quotes.text: When the outer ego, from the surface of its consciousness, reflects the outer world, it sees reflections of the inner ego which are the images within its own eye; and as the self creates matter subconsciously within its own eye, and as the self creates matter subconsciously and not consciously, and as the self creates matter in line with inner and not outer expectations, so then does the ego, in viewing the material universe, come face to face with the face of its own inner ego; and the outer ego cannot escape from this inner self.
quotes.translation: 当外在自我从其意识表面反映外在世界时，它看到的是内在自我的反映，这些反映是其自身眼中的影像；由于自我在自身眼中潜意识地而非有意识地创造物质，并且自我根据内在而非外在的期待创造物质，因此当自我审视物质宇宙时，便与内在自我的面貌面对面；外在自我无法逃离这个内在自我。

quotes.text: （This cancellation raised many questions, since Seth had said nothing about any such event; on the other hand, his forecasts had all been prefaced by the word "if," or similar ones. We thought briefly of asking for a special session to deal with the rather surprising turn of events, but decided against it, not wanting to cultivate any such dependent habits. My personal thought was that our expectations concerning the house had been in the process of change, the loss of the loan being the natural culmination of this.
quotes.translation: （这个取消引发了许多问题，因为赛斯从未提及任何此类事件；另一方面，他的预测都带有“如果”或类似词语作为前提。我们曾短暂考虑要求召开一次特别课来处理这个相当出人意料的事件转折，但最终决定不这么做，不想养成任何依赖性的习惯。我个人的想法是，我们对房子的期待一直在变化中，贷款的损失正是这种变化的自然结果。）

quotes.text: Experience is the product of the mind, the spirit, conscious thoughts and feelings, and unconscious thoughts and feelings. These together form the reality that you know. You are hardly at the mercy of a reality, therefore, that exists apart from yourself, or is thrust upon you. You are so intimately connected with the physical events composing your life experience that often you cannot distinguish between the seemingly material occurrences and the thoughts, expectations and desires that gave them birth.
quotes.translation: 经验是心智、灵性、有意识的思想和感受，以及无意识的思想和感受的产物。这些共同构成了你所知的实相。因此，你几乎不受任何独立于你之外或强加于你的实相所支配。你与构成你人生经历的物质事件如此紧密相连，以至于你常常无法区分看似物质的事件与赋予它们生命的思想、期待和欲望。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The type of dream, or the types of dreams experienced by any individual, is determined by many factors. I am speaking now of the dream experience as it occurs, and not of the remnant of it that his ego allows him to consciously recall. As an individual creates his physical image and environment according to his abilities and defects, and in line with his expectations and subconscious and inner needs, so does he create his dreams; and these interact with the outer environment which he has created.
quotes.translation: 梦境类型或个体经历的各种梦境，由诸多因素决定。我现在说的是梦境体验本身，而非自我允许意识回忆的残余部分。正如个体根据自身能力与缺陷，并依照其期望、潜意识及内在需求创造物质形象与环境，他同样创造着自己的梦境；而这些梦境又与他所创造的外在环境相互作用。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: You are bending over backward not to make money, both of you, though this is somewhat more understandable on Ruburt's part, at least since his training is not as specific. When either of you demand or request more money, you feel like thieves. Ruburt has yet to manage his abilities competently but you have except for this distortive expectation which colors your constructions.
quotes.translation: 你们两人都在刻意回避赚钱，虽然鲁柏的情况多少可以理解——至少他的训练不够专精。每当你们要求或请求更多金钱时，就会觉得自己像小偷。鲁柏尚未能妥善运用自身能力，而你也如此——除了这个扭曲的期望在影响你们的建构。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)

quotes.text: You possess within yourself all of those potentials in which consciousness creatively takes part. The cell does not need to be consciously aware of you in order to fulfill itself, even though your expectations of health largely influence its existence, but your recognition of the soul and entity can help you direct energies from these other dimensions into your daily life.
quotes.translation: 你内在拥有意识创造性参与的所有潜能。细胞无需有意识地认知你就能实现自身，尽管你对健康的期望极大影响着它的存续；但你对灵魂与存有的认知，能帮助你将这些其他维度的能量导向日常生活。

quotes.text: He did not want her to read the book, you see. He felt this left him open. His expectations, in other words, caused the attack, unfortunately. These feelings began when the book was definitely accepted. They did not grow into such actual disproportionate terms until the time of actual showing arrived.
quotes.translation: 你看，他不想让她读那本书。他感觉这让自己暴露无遗。换句话说，他的期望不幸引发了那次攻击。这些感受在书籍被明确接受时就开始滋生，直到实际展示时刻才膨胀到如此不成比例的程度。

quotes.text: You form the fabric of your experience through your own beliefs and expectations. These personal ideas about yourself and the nature of reality will affect your thoughts and emotions. You take your beliefs about reality as truth, and often do not question them. They seem self-explanatory. They appear in your mind as statements of fact, far too obvious for examination.
quotes.translation: 你通过自己的信念与期望编织经验之网。这些关于自我与实相本质的个人观念，会影响你的思想与情绪。你将关于实相的信念视为真理，往往不加质疑。它们看似不言自明，以事实陈述的姿态出现在你脑海中，显然无需检验。

quotes.text: Expectation on his part of the house to some degree at least provided this. His tendency, both for undisciplined reactions and for habitual discipline, seem contradictory, but represent merely counterbalances in his nature. He could, conceivably, act with no discipline, but he could also conceivably deny himself any freedom at all, and hide within schedule and habit.
quotes.translation: 他对房子的期望至少在一定程度上提供了这点。他既有无纪律反应的倾向，又有习惯性自律的倾向，看似矛盾，实则只是他天性中的平衡机制。可以想象，他可能完全不受约束地行动，也可能彻底剥夺自己任何自由，躲进日程与习惯之中。

quotes.text: （I spent eight or nine days in bed, the victim of what is generally called a virus. At no time however did I blame a virus, feeling that the real cause was psychic, thus permitting the virus to come to the fore. I was somewhat embarrassed at having "goofed" psychically. As time passed, and while I pored over several daily New York City newspapers plus Elmira's daily paper, I came to realize to a small extent that poor expectations on my part had much to do with my falling ill.
quotes.translation: 我在床上躺了八九天，成了通常所说的病毒的受害者。但我从未归咎于病毒，觉得真正的原因是心理层面的，这才让病毒有机可乘。我对自己在心理上"搞砸了"感到有些难堪。随着时间的推移，当我仔细翻阅几份纽约市日报和埃尔迈拉日报时，我才稍微意识到，我自身的负面期望与我的生病有很大关系。

quotes.text: Dreams do express a personality's basic reality. Negative dreams tend to reinforce the negative aspects of the personality, helping to form vicious circles of unfortunate complications. Upon suggestion dream actions can be turned toward fulfilling constructive expectations, which can themselves effect a definite change for the better in the personality involved.
quotes.translation: 梦确实表达了一个人格的基本实相。负面的梦往往会强化人格的负面面向，助长不幸纠葛的恶性循环。通过暗示，梦的行为可以转向实现建设性的期望，这些期望本身就能对相关人格产生明确的改善效果。

The gallery represents a unit of community affiliation in which he can exert some power, and yet be within a community unit.
quotes.translation: 于是他试图将自己裹在组织的斗篷里。但他本质上仍然保持匿名。他归属的渴望和期望也变得更加强烈。画廊代表了一个社区归属的单位，在其中他可以施展一些权力，同时又处于一个社区单位之内。

quotes.text: Your thoughts and beliefs and desires form the events that you view on television. If you want to change your world, you must first change your thoughts, expectations, and beliefs. If every reader of this book changed his or her attitudes, even though not one law was rewritten, tomorrow the world would have changed for the better. The new laws would follow.
quotes.translation: 你的思想、信念和欲望形成了你在电视上看到的事件。如果你想改变你的世界，你必须首先改变你的思想、期望和信念。如果这本书的每一位读者都改变自己的态度，即使没有一条法律被重写，明天世界也会变得更好。新的法律会随之而来。

quotes.text: A belief in hell fires can cause you to hallucinate Hades' conditions. A belief in a stereotyped heaven can result in a hallucination of heavenly conditions. You always form your own reality according to your ideas and expectations. This is the nature of consciousness in whatever reality it finds itself. Such hallucinations, I assure you, are temporary.
quotes.translation: 对地狱之火的信念可能导致你幻觉出冥界的状况。对刻板天堂的信念可能导致对天堂状况的幻觉。你总是根据你的观念和期望形成你自己的实相。这是意识在任何它所处的实相中的本质。我向你保证，这样的幻觉是暂时的。

quotes.text: The subconscious, as you call it, represents a tremendous raw power that triggers forth into construction according to the expectations which you form from the emotions. The intellect should help you understand this power plant, so that you can switch your power where it is needed. The intellect should operate like an x-ray, enabling you to see inward.
quotes.translation: 所谓的潜意识代表了一种巨大的原始力量，它会根据你从情绪中形成的期望而触发建设。理智应该帮助你理解这个发电厂，这样你就可以在需要的地方切换你的力量。理智应该像X光一样运作，让你能够向内看。

quotes.translation: 它在学习物质感知的方法，内在体验你的反应，并熟悉你运用物质感官获取信息的方式。它正在以或多或少忠实于所需样貌的复制品来构建自己的身体。（停顿）它使用你自身的许多期望作为模型，也使用其父亲的许多期望。

quotes.translation: 被携带的期望。或许比其他任何特质更能彰显个体特性，并代表其人格最内在的面向。它是物质建构的框架，比原子和分子更能代表构筑其建构的心灵积木。

quotes.text: Between lives an individual may see flashes of the future existence, not necessarily of particular events, but experience the essence of the new relationship and in expectation remind himself of the challenge he has set. In these terms, the ghosts of the future are as real in your homes as the ghosts of the past.
quotes.translation: 在生命之间，个体可能瞥见未来存在的片段——未必是特定事件，而是体验新关系的本质，并在期望中提醒自己已设定的挑战。就这些层面而言，未来之灵与过去之灵在你们家中同样真实。

quotes.text: We have spoken of expectations. These, you see, are electrical realities, which may explain their importance; for you not only sometimes predict so- called future events, but you create their actuality within the electrical field, and therefore insure their existence one way or another within the physical field.
quotes.translation: 我们曾谈到过期望。你看，这些期望是电性的实相，这或许能解释它们的重要性；因为你不仅有时预测所谓的未来事件，还在电性场域内创造了它们的实相，从而确保它们以某种方式在物质场域中存在。

quotes.text: Suggestion then can shape the future. Expectation enters in here particularly of course. Suggestion can shape dreams, and the dreams themselves then operate as action. A strong dream can be a more significant psychic action than any physical experience, and it can change the course of the personality completely.
quotes.translation: 暗示因此可以塑造未来。当然，期望在此特别重要。暗示可以塑造梦境，而梦境本身则作为行动运作。一个强烈的梦境可能比任何物质体验都更具意义的心理行动，并能完全改变人格的轨迹。

quotes.text: As long as you believe this so will your expectations of reality become, in truth, reality. quotes.translation: 只要你相信这一点，你对实相的期望就会真的成为实相。不言而喻，如果你所有的精力都投入赚钱，就很少会留给绘画，但这与“艺术家必须贫穷”的等式之间还有很大距离。

quotes.text: You make your reality from your expectations, and this is one of the greatest truths. I can tell you no better. You must deal with the realities that you have made, or change them. There are no alternatives. I cannot make you believe anything, and I would not if I could. This is not my purpose.
quotes.translation: 你从你的期望中创造你的实相，这是最伟大的真理之一。我无法告诉你更好的了。你必须处理你已经创造的实相，或者改变它们。没有其他选择。我无法让你相信任何事，即使我能，我也不会这样做。这不是我的目的。

quotes.text: Within your system expectation indeed forms your own physical environment. This does not mean that the rich, for example, have attained to spiritual status necessarily. The personal environment taken as a whole, with interpersonal relationships, is an indicator of the inner situation, however.
quotes.translation: 在你的系统内，期望确实形成了你自己的物质环境。但这并不意味着，例如，富人必然达到了灵性状态。然而，将个人环境作为一个整体来看，包括人际关系，是内在状况的一个指标。

quotes.text: Aggressions are merely the result of energy not clearly directed, invalid survival patterns. If these aggressions are not handled with some degree of success, they will form themselves into expectations, where they will then let forth their power in the formation of unfortunate constructions.
quotes.translation: 攻击行为仅仅是能量未明确导向、无效生存模式的结果。若这些攻击行为未能以某种程度成功处理，它们将凝聚成期望，继而释放其力量，形成不幸的建构。

quotes.text: Your expectations must change first. On a limited level, commitment to intangible values, which you have, limits you to a commitment in physical terms. The two seem in opposition. quotes.translation: 你的期望必须首先改变。在有限层面上，对无形价值的承诺——正如你所拥有的——将你限制于物质层面的承诺。两者看似对立。这是由于早期环境力量导致的心灵扩展与理解不足所致。

quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: We have already spoken of psychic constructions, and we have said that physical shapes are built upon inner psychic frameworks. Emotions then, in their own realm unperceived by the outer senses, have their own solidity, shape, and it is from these that your expectations are formed.
quotes.translation: 我们已讨论过心灵建构，并说过物质形态是建立在内在心灵框架上的。因此，情感在其自身领域内虽不为外在感官所感知，却拥有自身的坚实性与形态，而你的期望正是由此形成。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: There are interactions here of utmost importance. For simplicity's sake you may say that ideas are electric, in that they spark enzymes within the mechanism, that then automatically begin the work of physical construction, in line with inner expectations as outlined earlier.
quotes.translation: 此处存在至关重要的相互作用。为简化起见，你可以说观念是电性的，因为它们能在身体机制中激发酶素，随即自动开始物质建构的工作，与先前概述的内在期望保持一致。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: I am speaking in highly practical terms. Your expectations literally create your life as you know it. Your attitude toward transportation needs adjustment. As long as you believe firmly that you cannot afford the kind of automobile that you want, this will be literally true.
quotes.translation: 我是在极其实际的层面上谈论。你的期望确实创造了你所知的生活。你对交通方式的态度需要调整。只要你坚信自己买不起想要的那种汽车，这就会成为事实。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: This is a direct result of the results of inner expectations, and begins to yield its fruits. Changes in your living rhythm will no longer confront you in terms of either bitterness, anger or regret, for you are creating a more constructive material and psychic environment.
quotes.translation: 这是内在期望结果的直接产物，并开始结出果实。你生活节奏的改变将不再以苦涩、愤怒或遗憾的形式呈现，因为你正在创造一个更具建设性的物质与心灵环境。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

source_raw.title: （Sam Levine knows about Seth but has never attended a session. He has been invited to. He has expressed neither belief nor disbelief, he has also listened to part of the tape recording we made of Jane when delivering the 25th session. See page 183. Someday Jane and I intend to put the pressure on Sam and make him attend a session, no matter how busy he is. [ Session 25 is in Vol. 1.]）

quotes.text: I suggest something. I will say good night. If I feel like going on for hours, it is because one year ago tonight I first made contact with Ruburt--at least a subconscious initial contact of which he was not consciously aware. The year has been a good one, even though my practical suggestions have not been followed, your situation has improved in line with the laws of expectation.
quotes.translation: 我建议到此为止。我要道晚安了。若我兴致勃勃想继续谈上几小时，那是因为一年前的今晚，我首次与鲁柏建立联系——至少是潜意识层面的初步接触，而他当时并未察觉。这一年过得不错，尽管我的实用建议未被采纳，但你们的情况已遵循期望法则有所改善。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: Matter in your field is undifferentiated by nature, so to speak, and it is the complicated human abilities that give it form and meaning. I have mentioned that expectation has much to do with individual constructions, and indeed this cannot be stressed too strongly.
quotes.translation: 你们场域中的物质本质上未分化，是复杂的人类能力赋予其形态与意义。我曾提及期望与个体建构密切相关，这一点再怎么强调也不为过。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: This characteristic of materializing thoughts and emotions into physical realities is an attribute of the soul. Now in your reality, these thoughts are made physical. In other realities, they may be "constructed" in an entirely different fashion. So your soul, that which you are, constructs your physical daily reality for you from the nature of your thoughts and expectations.
quotes.translation: 将思想与情感物质化为物理实相的特性，是灵魂的固有属性。在你们的实相中，这些思想被转化为物质形态；而在其他实相中，它们可能以全然不同的方式被"建构"。因此，你们的灵魂——即你们真正的本质——正根据你们的思想与期望，为你们建构着日常的物理实相。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: If you became wealthy, you would then be in danger of losing your ability, since in your realm of expectation ability of this nature and wealth do not exist simultaneously. To protect your ability then, you would rigorously fight to retain your poverty.
quotes.translation: 若你变得富有，反而会危及你的能力，因为在你的期望领域里，这种性质的能力与财富无法并存。为守护能力，你会竭力固守贫穷。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: On a practical level, later, we will go into steps that can be taken to offset such an unfortunate liability. Indeed, you have both yourselves begun to operate much more efficiently in these important areas of expectation and construction.
quotes.translation: 在实践层面，我们稍后将探讨抵消这种不利倾向的具体步骤。事实上，你们两人已开始在期望与建构这些重要领域更高效地运作。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: You have helped others as well as yourselves. Any seeming caution on Ruburt's part has been necessary, particularly in the beginning. This caution will gradually be replaced by expectation and confidence, but it is a necessary element.
quotes.translation: 你们既帮助了他人，也帮助了自己。鲁柏表现出的任何谨慎都是必要的，尤其在初期阶段。这份谨慎将逐渐被期望与信心取代，但它仍是不可或缺的要素。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: Expectation is the force, then, that triggers psychic realities into physical construction.
quotes.translation: 期待，便是触发心灵实相转化为物质构造的力量。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: The mind constructs into matter its own idea of reality, and this is where personal expectation comes into play. You do not only create your own environment, generally speaking, you create it concretely, in forms of mass and matter.
quotes.translation: 心灵将自身对实相的观念构建为物质，而个人期待在此发挥作用。一般而言，你不仅创造自己的环境，更以质量与物质的具体形式创造它。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

source_raw.title: （Jane's Jan. 10/64 trance is described in Session 14, Vol. 1.）

quotes.text: Since the individual constructs matter, and indeed constructs his own physical universe, he can improve these constructions; and his expectations are intimately connected with the subconscious mechanism of construction itself.
quotes.translation: 既然个体构建物质，甚至构建自己的物质宇宙，他就能改善这些构建；而他的期待与构建本身的潜意识机制紧密相连。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

source_raw.title: （Jane's Jan. 10/64 trance is described in Session 14, Vol. 1.）

quotes.text: Since this sort of expectation allies you both, you both should try to overcome it, since if one doesn't there would be a troublesome, though not critical, discrepancy that could at least temporarily bother your relationship.
quotes.translation: 由于这类期待将你们双方联结，你们都应努力克服它，因为若有一方未能做到，便会产生虽不致命却令人困扰的差异，至少会暂时影响你们的关系。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: Your expectations alone will change your material circumstances. The belief that you can afford the kind of car you require will in itself form the circumstances that will make such an automobile literally possible.
quotes.translation: 仅凭你的期待就能改变物质环境。你相信自己买得起所需车型的信念，本身就会形成让这辆车真正成为可能的境遇。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: We here have been concerned with expectation, and the way it influences the environment, and the actual world that you create for yourself. This is far from Pollyanna hogwash. It is practical, and it works.
quotes.translation: 我们在此探讨的是期待，以及它如何影响环境，影响你为自己创造的实际世界。这绝非波莉安娜式的空谈。它切实可行，且行之有效。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: Nevertheless again, you would have made an excellent project of it; both of you changed your expectations, when faced by them in concrete terms. I will not go into this now. This is a short postscript only.
quotes.translation: 然而，你们本可以把它做得非常出色；当面对具体状况时，你们两人都改变了预期。我现在不深入探讨这个，这只是一个简短的附言。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: Enough cannot be said along these lines regarding the nature of your expectations, for according to the manner in which you expect your reality to behave, in that manner shall it be.
quotes.translation: 关于你们期望的本质，无论怎样强调都不为过，因为你们以何种方式期待现实呈现，现实便会以何种方式呈现。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: There is no event upon the face of the earth in which each of you has not played some part, however minute, because of the nature of your thoughts, beliefs, and expectations.
quotes.translation: 地球上发生的任何事件，你们每个人都或多或少参与其中，无论多么微小，这都是由你们的思想、信念和期望的本质所决定的。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (Over two years ago, in Note 2 for Session 801, and in the opening notes for Session 805, I described our decision to add the writing room to the house. All four of us involved in the moving were quite amused, then -- for the friends who helped Jane and me carry the furniture yesterday are the father-and-son contracting team that built the room in the first place.
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: He taught himself the hard way, in other words, out of a lack of faith and expectation.
quotes.translation: 换句话说，他是通过缺乏信心和期望的艰难方式自我学习的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: --because you will understand that fearful expectations greatly color your perception of reality, and your construction of it.
quotes.translation: ——因为你会明白，恐惧的期望会极大地扭曲你对现实的感知，以及你对现实的构建。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: There is more to it than this, but this is the basic reason. The added expectation of cold weather helped.
quotes.translation: 这其中还有更多含义，但这是基本原因。对寒冷天气的额外期望也起了作用。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: Now: Your conscious ideas, expectations and beliefs direct the health and activity of the cells. Period.
quotes.translation: 现在：你有意识的观念、期待与信念，直接引导着细胞的健康与活动。句号。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The emotions indeed do form the expectations, and it is not the other way around.
quotes.translation: 情绪确实形成了期待，而非反过来。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
